title: TeraWulf Earns More From AI Computing Than Bitcoin Mining in Q1
text: TeraWulf reported Q1 2026 revenues totaling $34 million, with the historic milestone that AI computing and HPC leasing ($21 million) surpassed Bitcoin mining revenue ($13 million) for the first time. This revenue inflection represents a fundamental shift in TeraWulf's business model and validates the company's pivot strategy. The 117% quarter-over-quarter growth in HPC revenue demonstrates strong demand for TeraWulf's AI compute capacity and the company's ability to secure high-value, long-term contracts. Bitcoin mining revenue declined approximately 50% sequentially, reflecting the company's deliberate shift in capital allocation and operational focus toward higher-margin, more stable HPC leasing. The milestone represents validation of management's strategy to leverage the company's substantial power infrastructure and existing data centers to participate in the rapidly growing AI compute market. With 522 critical IT MW under contract and 250-500 MW targeted annually, TeraWulf is positioning itself as a leading provider of AI compute infrastructure.
